edges.description: Defective collagen biosynthesis and fibrillogenesis reduce tensile strength of ECM-rich tissues.
Structurally weak connective tissues fail under routine mechanical stress.
Lax ligaments and capsules increase range of motion beyond normal limits.
Reduced dermal collagen integrity permits excessive skin stretch.
Vessel wall fragility increases susceptibility to spontaneous arterial tearing.
Valve connective tissue weakness predisposes to leaflet redundancy and prolapse.
Abnormal stromal collagen architecture contributes to progressive corneal ectasia.
Collagen defects impair closure strength and scar maturation.
Chronically unstable joints are prone to repeated subluxation and dislocation.
Recurrent tissue injury and instability drive persistent musculoskeletal pain.
Visceral connective tissue laxity can compromise gastroesophageal barrier competence.
Altered connective tissue-bone crosstalk contributes to reduced skeletal mineralization.
Soft tissue laxity and impaired force transmission contribute to reduced baseline tone.
Abnormal mechanosignaling reduces tissue resilience during repetitive loading.
Dysregulated integrin signaling impairs coordinated repair responses.
Persistent inflammatory mechanotransduction amplifies pain signaling in unstable joints.
Impaired proteostasis worsens collagen and matrix protein quality in fibroblasts.
Chronic cellular stress in vascular connective tissue exacerbates vessel wall vulnerability.
Proteolytic collagen degradation further weakens already abnormal extracellular matrix.
Accelerated ECM turnover increases susceptibility to repetitive tissue injury.

evidence.reference_title: [Vascular Ehlers-Danlos syndrome].
evidence.snippet: The mutation of the COL3A1 gene which encodes type III collagen, is responsible of early vascular (spontaneous arterial rupture or dissection), digestive (perforation) and obstetrical events (uterine and arterial rupture)

evidence.reference_title: Vascular Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome: Long-Term Observational Study.
evidence.snippet: The only published clinical trial to date demonstrated the benefit of celiprolol on arterial morbimortality.

nodes.description: Vascular EDS fibroblasts exhibit impaired autophagy-lysosome pathways and cellular stress responses, contributing to abnormal ECM homeostasis and protein quality control.
Mutations in genes encoding collagen or collagen-modifying enzymes lead to defective collagen synthesis and assembly.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular defects in the Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Several abnormalities in collagen biosynthesis have been described in patients with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome. Examples of collagen structural mutations as well as post-translational enzymatic defects have been detected.

evidence.reference_title: Patient-derived extracellular matrix demonstrates role of COL3A1 in blood vessel mechanics.
evidence.snippet: Vascular Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome (vEDS) is a rare autosomal dominant disease caused by mutations in the COL3A1 gene.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in collagen genes: causes of rare and some common diseases in humans.
evidence.snippet: Recently, several mutations in three other collagen genes (COL2A1, COL3A1, and COL4A5) have been found in probands with genetic diseases involving tissues rich in these collagens.

evidence.reference_title: A frameshift variant in the COL5A1 gene in a cat with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Human EDS may be caused by variants in several different genes including COL5A1, which encodes the collagen type V alpha 1 chain.

evidence.reference_title: Bi-allelic AEBP1 mutations in two patients with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Bi-allelic loss-of-function mutations in the adipocyte enhancer-binding protein 1 (AEBP1) gene were reported in three families with an autosomal recessive EDS-like condition.

nodes.description: Intracellular activation of complement C1r/C1s proteases leads to extracellular activated C1s that degrades collagen I, destabilizing the periodontal ECM in periodontal EDS.
Abnormal collagen leads to weakened connective tissues throughout the body.

evidence.reference_title: Heritable disorders of connective tissue: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The cardinal features are cutaneous hyperextensibility, joint hypermobility, bleeding diathesis, and tissue fragility, and these features lead to a large variety of additional manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Degradation of collagen I by activated C1s in periodontal Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: pEDS is caused by heterozygous missense mutations in C1R and C1S genes of the classical complement C1 complex.

evidence.reference_title: Defects in the biochemistry of collagen in diseases of connective tissue.
evidence.snippet: Any defect in the normal mechanisms responsible for the synthesis and secretion of collagen molecules or the deposition of these molecules into extracellular fibers could result in abnormal fibrillogenesis; such defects could result in a connective tissue disease.

evidence.reference_title: An update on the new classification of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and review of the causes of bleeding in this population.
evidence.snippet: Collagen, which forms the framework of vessel walls, is altered in many patients with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS) leading to weakening of the vessel wall or the supporting tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers Danlos syndrome and gastrointestinal manifestations: a 20-year experience at Mayo Clinic.
evidence.snippet: Commonest GI symptoms were: abdominal pain (56.1%), nausea (42.3%), constipation (38.6%), heartburn (37.6%), and irritable bowel syndrome-like symptoms (27.5%).

evidence.reference_title: Enrichment of Patients With Ehlers Danlos Syndrome in Idiopathic Gastroparesis-A Gene Set Enrichment Analysis.
evidence.snippet: We report a significant enrichment of EDS cases in a set of patients with gastroparesis.

evidence.reference_title: Respiratory manifestations in the Ehlers-Danlos syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Functional aerodigestive manifestations such as inducible laryngeal obstruction may be misdiagnosed as asthma, with gastro-esophageal dysmotility and reflux as common contributing factors.

nodes.description: Defective collagen matrix leads to altered integrin signaling, particularly upregulation of integrin αvβ3, contributing to persistent inflammation and impaired wound healing in classical EDS.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes, Joint Hypermobility and Hypermobility Spectrum Disorders.
evidence.snippet: Currently, musculoskeletal manifestations related to joint hypermobility are perceived as the most prevalent determinants of the quality of life of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Relevance of Joint Hypermobility and Its Impact on Musculoskeletal Pain and Bone Mass.
evidence.snippet: Increasing data demonstrate that pain is a major disability determinator in JH and EDS. Recent findings confirm a complex pathogenesis for pain in JH and EDS and suggest a potential role for joint instability, central sensitization and small fiber neuropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence and quality of temporomandibular disorders, chronic pain and psychological distress in patients with classical and hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome: an exploratory study.
evidence.snippet: The Ehlers-Danlos syndromes are a group of clinically and genetically heterogeneous hereditary diseases affecting the connective tissue. They are characterized by hypermobility of the joints, hyperextensible skin and friable tissue.

evidence.reference_title: Classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002761 | Generalized joint laxity | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers-Danlos syndromes and their manifestations in the visual system.
evidence.snippet: In addition to these commonly recognized phenotypes, recent studies have notably highlighted variable ophthalmic features in EDS.

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers Danlos syndrome, kyphoscoliotic type due to Lysyl Hydroxylase 1 deficiency in two children without congenital or early onset kyphoscoliosis.
evidence.snippet: As the two patients reported here illustrate, patients with kEDS-PLOD1 do not always have a kyphoscoliosis present at birth or in the first year of life, neither do they necessarily develop kyphoscoliosis later in infancy.

evidence.reference_title: Keratoconus.
evidence.snippet: Keratoconus is most commonly an isolated disorder, although several reports describe an association with Down syndrome, Leber's congenital amaurosis, and mitral valve prolapse.

evidence.reference_title: Brittle cornea syndrome and its delineation from the kyphoscoliotic type of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS VI): report on 23 patients and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The brittle cornea syndrome (BCS) is a generalized connective tissue disorder characterized by corneal rupture following only minor trauma, keratoconus or keratoglobus, blue sclerae, hyperelasticity of the skin without excessive fragility, and hypermobility of the joints.

evidence.reference_title: Keratoconus tomographic indices in osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: A high proportion of patients had tomographically suspect corneas when using keratoconus diagnostic indices

evidence.reference_title: Cardiovascular manifestations of hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and hypermobility spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: Mitral valve prolapse was present in 7.5% and thoracic aortic dilatation in 15.2%.

evidence.reference_title: Mitral valve prolapse.
evidence.snippet: It appears to occur more frequently in females and occasionally it is familial... particularly Marfan's syndrome, rheumatic heart disease, coronary heart disease, congestive cardiomyopathy, ostium secundum atrial septal defect, Ehlers-Danlos syndrome or abnormalities of the thoracic cage.

evidence.reference_title: The Heart Muscle and Valve Involvement in Marfan Syndrome, Loeys-Dietz Syndromes, and Collagenopathies.
evidence.snippet: There is substantial symptoms overlap between the EDS subtypes, and they are associated with an increased incidence of cardiovascular abnormalities, such as mitral valve prolapse and aortic dissection.

evidence.reference_title: Utilization of echocardiography in Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Mitral valve prolapse and bicuspid aortic valve occurred at the same incidence as the general population.

evidence.reference_title: A "hyperextensive" review of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS) is a heterogeneous group of connective tissue disorders characterized by hyperextensibility, delayed wound healing, joint hypermobility, thin skin, easy bruising, tissue fragility, 'cigarette-paper' scarring over bony prominences, mitral valve prolapse, and other findings.

evidence.reference_title: Cutaneous wound healing in Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Although delayed wound healing has been reported to be a complication of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome in humans, using clinical and histologic criteria, wound healing in dogs and cats with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome appears to be similar to nonaffected animals.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic aspects of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type.
evidence.snippet: Classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a heritable connective tissue disorder characterized by skin hyperextensibility, fragile and soft skin, delayed wound healing with formation of atrophic scars, easy bruising, and generalized joint hypermobility.

evidence.reference_title: Classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0001058 | Poor wound healing | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Heritable disorders of connective tissue: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The cardinal features are cutaneous hyperextensibility, joint hypermobility, bleeding diathesis, and tissue fragility...

evidence.reference_title: Atypical COL3A1 variants (glutamic acid to lysine) cause vascular Ehlers-Danlos syndrome with a consistent phenotype of tissue fragility and skin hyperextensibility.
evidence.snippet: All individuals with these atypical variants exhibited skin hyperextensibility as seen in individuals with classical EDS and classical-like EDS...

evidence.reference_title: Localized Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Hyperextensibility of the skin in this region developed within the subsequent five years...

evidence.reference_title: The Many Facets of Hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Of the 13 subtypes of Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes (EDSs)...the authors provide an overview of hEDS symptoms and... current treatment options.

evidence.reference_title: Dermal changes in Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Collagen fibrils showed a distorted arrangement... abnormal collagen fibrils in normal skin suggests one of eight types of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Classical Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0000974 | Hyperextensible skin | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Fragile tissues are prone to damage, leading to manifestations like joint dislocations, organ ruptures, and poor wound healing.

evidence.reference_title: The Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes comprise a heterogeneous group of diseases, which are characterized by fragility of the soft connective tissues and widespread manifestations in skin, ligaments and joints, blood vessels and internal organs... The clinical spectrum varies from mild skin and joint hyperlaxity to severe physical disability and life-threatening vascular complications.

evidence.reference_title: Arterial fragility in kyphoscoliotic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Arterial fragility is an important feature of the disease... Arterial fragility is an important characteristic of kyphoscoliotic EDS. It manifests as spontaneous arterial rupture, dissections and dissecting aneurysms.

evidence.reference_title: Bleeding in the heritable connective tissue disorders: mechanisms, diagnosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Easy bruising and bleeding are prominent features of some heritable disorders of connective tissue (HDCT), resulting from fragility of capillaries and the perivascular connective tissue... In the vascular subtype of EDS, caused by defects in type III collagen, fragility of vessel walls can lead to life-threatening bleeding and premature death.
